creator: kris@obsecurity.org
text:
On Fri, Aug 31, 2001 at 02:22:55PM -0700, Paul English wrote:

> 	Adaptec 2940UW controller on Dual PIII w/Tyan S1834 motherboard, 1 GB PC133RAM. 1 internal IDE HDD, and 1 external SCSI-IDE RAID 5 array. Arena 9000EX model.
> 
> >Description:
> 
> 	When a certain (custom C) program is run which reads and writes a great deal to the array the following error occurs:
> 	AHC0; AHC_INTR - referenced SCB not valid during SELTO SCB(15, 12)
> 	usually repeated many times followed quickly by a system crash.
> 
> >How-To-Repeat:
> 
> 	Difficult/impossible to repeat outside of our environment.

I wonder how you expect anyone to fix this, then.

Kris
